forecast-period.text: A trough over inland WA is expected to move across western and northern SA on Sunday and continues northeast early in the week. A ridge of high pressure persists over the south. A cold front clips the southeast early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers, about the north and west. tending to rain about the far northwest late. Chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Maximum temperatures above average, tending near to below average in the north and southwest. Southeast to northeasterly winds, with local sea breezes.
Possible showers and the chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Dry elsewhere. East to northeasterly winds, with local sea breezes.

Showers, extending statewide, tending to rain about the north with locally heavy falls possible. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperature below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Showers contracting to the pastoral districts. Rain at times about the north with locally heavy falls possible. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperature below average. Southeast to easterly winds.

text.p: Showers about the pastoral and Flinders districts. Rain at times about the northeast with locally heavy falls possible. Maximum temperature below average. Southeast to easterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Broadly 5mm, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the northern agricultural area, 15 to 30 mm about the south of Pastoral districts and far west, reaching 50 to 100 mm about the north, with possible isolated falls greater than 100 mm.
